mnemonics.title: SCALER Benefits
mnemonics.content:
S - Scalability (scale up/down as needed)
C - Cost-efficiency (pay-as-you-go model)
A - Accessibility (access from anywhere)
L - Reliability (99.9% uptime, backups)
E - Elasticity (auto-scale resources)
R - Resource Pooling (shared resources)

mnemonics.title: SADDLE Limitations
mnemonics.content:
S - Security (data breach risks)
A - Availability (downtime issues)
D - Dependency (internet required)
D - Data Privacy (compliance concerns)
L - Limited Control (vendor manages infrastructure)
E - Exit/Lock-in (difficult to switch providers)

mnemonics.title: FLEXIBLE Benefits
mnemonics.content:
F - Fast Deployment
L - Lower maintenance
E - Easy collaboration
X - X-scalability (unlimited scaling)
I - Improved reliability
L - Lower upfront costs
E - Eco-friendly (energy efficient)

acronyms.usage: When remembering cloud service models - IaaS provides virtualized computing resources
When remembering cloud service models - PaaS provides development platforms
When remembering cloud service models - SaaS delivers software applications over the internet
Traditional IT model - large upfront investments in hardware
Cloud model - pay-as-you-go, ongoing operational costs
Contract guaranteeing uptime (e.g., 99.9%) - important for reliability benefits

visualTips.visualization: Imagine an elastic rubber band stretching - resources expand to match demand, then shrink back
Think of a shared office workspace with hot desks - multiple users share the same physical resources dynamically
Picture a taxi meter - you only pay for the distance (resources) you actually use
Imagine your data in a bank vault in a remote location - protected but you don't physically control it
Think of a hotel with unique room keys - switching hotels means re-learning everything
Public = restaurant (open to all), Private = home kitchen (exclusive), Hybrid = buffet (mix of both)

analogies.analogy: Like keeping money in a bank (cloud) vs under your mattress (local). Banks offer security, accessibility, and backup but you rely on their services.
Like a smart traffic signal that adds more lanes during rush hour and reduces them at night - automatically adjusts to demand
Like an ATM - you get resources instantly without talking to a human, any time of day
Like an apartment building - multiple tenants share the same building (physical infrastructure) but have separate, private spaces
Like a waterfall-powered mill - if the water stops flowing (internet), production stops completely
Like sending confidential letters through a courier service - you're trusting a third party with sensitive information
